rawText:
When Iran effectively closed the Strait of Hormuz in retaliation for American and Israeli air strikes in early March 2026, it detonated what may prove to be the most severe energy supply shock in half a century -- one that has sent the price of Brent crude surging past $115 a barrel, disrupted roughly 15% of global oil supply, and exposed the fragility of an interconnected world economy that had, until recently, taken the unimpeded flow of Gulf commodities for granted.

Although the world's dependence on oil has diminished since the Arab embargo of 1973 and the Iranian revolution of 1979, when crude was still commonly burned to generate electricity, this apparent resilience is deceptive, for today's oil demand -- concentrated in transport and petrochemicals -- is far more inelastic, meaning that prices must climb steeply to bring consumption into line with a supply disruption of this unprecedented magnitude, one that some analysts suggest could push crude beyond $150 a barrel if the strait remains closed.

The damage extends well beyond oil, reaching into commodities that few outside specialist trading desks had associated with the Persian Gulf: Qatar's liquefied natural gas hub at Ras Laffan, which supplied nearly a fifth of global LNG before a drone strike forced its closure, has dragged European gas prices up by more than half, while the loss of the facility's helium output -- roughly a third of worldwide supply and an irreplaceable coolant in semiconductor manufacturing -- threatens to bottleneck the very technology sector on which future economic growth depends.

Refined fuel markets, squeezed from both ends by the near-disappearance of Gulf crude and the inability of the region's mega-refineries to export, have deteriorated so sharply that Asian processors have cut throughput by up to 15%, China has suspended all refined-product exports, and modelling by the data firm Kpler suggests that, should Hormuz stay blocked, Oceania will exhaust 80% of its jet-fuel stocks within 36 days and poorer Asian nations will face critical petrol shortages in under a fortnight.

Manufacturing, the second pillar of global industry now buckling under the strain, depends on Gulf petrochemical plants for nearly 45% of seaborne naphtha flows and up to 30% of key plastic feedstocks -- a dependence so acute that several Asian plastic-makers have already declared force majeure, while pharmaceutical supply chains stretching from China's bulk chemical importers to India's generic-drug factories confront shortages of the active compounds that underpin everything from aspirin to antibiotics.

The aluminium market illustrates how a single chokepoint can ripple through the industrial world: with Qatar's mega-smelter starved of gas, Bahraini and Emirati plants unable to export, and shipping costs from Oman's port soaring under the threat of attack, three-month delivery prices on the London Metal Exchange have surged to near four-year highs, hammering the packaging, transport, and renewable-energy sectors that consume the metal in vast quantities.

Perhaps most ominously for the world's poorest nations, the war threatens global food production, given that a third of seaborne fertiliser trade -- chiefly urea derived from natural gas and phosphate -- transits the Strait of Hormuz, and countries such as Sudan, which sources over half its fertiliser from the Gulf, face the prospect of catastrophic shortages just as spring planting begins across the northern hemisphere.

Even the world's skies have contracted, as the conflict has rendered the airspace above the Persian Gulf all but impassable -- reducing Dubai's departures to a third of normal levels and Doha's to a mere 5% -- and forced airlines onto costly detours that add hours to east-west routes already lengthened by the closure of Russian airspace after the invasion of Ukraine, shrinking the corridor of passable sky to a sliver above Azerbaijan.

Central banks now confront a policy nightmare in which the inflationary pressures of soaring energy costs collide with the recessionary drag of supply-chain paralysis, leaving politicians to weigh the politically irresistible lure of energy subsidies -- which exceeded 2.5% of GDP in many European countries during the 2022 crisis and would, if repeated, deepen sovereign debts while shifting the worst pain to developing nations in Asia and Africa.

What the war has made inescapably clear is that an extraordinary share of the world's supply chains -- in oil, gas, petrochemicals, metals, fertiliser, and even the helium that cools the magnets inside chip-fabrication equipment -- funnels through a 54-kilometre-wide channel alongside a belligerent state, and that the era of assuming this vulnerability could be safely ignored has, with devastating abruptness, come to an end.

overview.title: The Economic Shockwave of the Iran War

sentences.original: When Iran effectively closed the Strait of Hormuz in retaliation for American and Israeli air strikes in early March 2026, it detonated what may prove to be the most severe energy supply shock in half a century -- one that has sent the price of Brent crude surging past $115 a barrel, disrupted roughly 15% of global oil supply, and exposed the fragility of an interconnected world economy that had, until recently, taken the unimpeded flow of Gulf commodities for granted.
sentences.translation: 2026년 3월 초 미국과 이스라엘의 공습에 대한 보복으로 이란이 호르무즈 해협을 사실상 봉쇄했을 때, 그것은 반세기 만에 가장 심각한 에너지 공급 충격으로 판명될 수 있는 사태를 촉발했다 -- 브렌트유 가격을 배럴당 115달러 이상으로 치솟게 하고, 세계 석유 공급의 약 15%를 교란시키며, 최근까지 페르시아만 원자재의 자유로운 흐름을 당연시해 온 상호 연결된 세계 경제의 취약성을 드러낸 충격이다.

grammarElements.text: When Iran effectively closed the Strait of Hormuz
grammarElements.label: 시간 부사절

grammarElements.text: what may prove to be the most severe energy supply shock
grammarElements.label: 명사절 목적어

grammarElements.text: one that has sent ... disrupted ... and exposed
grammarElements.label: 동격 관계절 (세 동사 병렬)

vocabulary.example: The sanctions were imposed in retaliation for the cyberattack.
Trade flows remained unimpeded despite rising tensions.

expressions.usage: 오랫동안 별 문제 없이 누려 온 것이 사실 취약했음을 지적할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: When [actor] [action], it detonated what may prove to be [consequence]
patterns.explanation: 어떤 행위가 역사적 규모의 결과를 촉발했음을 강조하는 도입 패턴
examples: When the bank collapsed, it triggered what may prove to be the worst financial crisis in decades.
When the dam broke, it unleashed what may prove to be the costliest natural disaster on record.

drillQuestions.question: When Iran effectively closed the Strait of Hormuz in ______ for American and Israeli air strikes, it detonated what may prove to be the most severe energy supply shock.

sentences.notes: 기사의 도입부로, 하나의 긴 문장 안에 시간 배경, 원인, 결과 세 가지를 동격절과 병렬 구조로 압축한다. detonated라는 폭발 은유가 핵심.

koreanAlignment.en: When Iran effectively closed the Strait of Hormuz
koreanAlignment.ko: 이란이 호르무즈 해협을 사실상 봉쇄했을 때

koreanAlignment.en: in retaliation for American and Israeli air strikes
the most severe energy supply shock in half a century
koreanAlignment.ko: 반세기 만에 가장 심각한 에너지 공급 충격

koreanAlignment.en: exposed the fragility of an interconnected world economy
koreanAlignment.ko: 상호 연결된 세계 경제의 취약성을 드러냈다

koreanAlignment.en: taken the unimpeded flow of Gulf commodities for granted
koreanAlignment.ko: 페르시아만 원자재의 자유로운 흐름을 당연시해 왔다

pronunciationNotes: Hormuz: /hɔːrˈmuːz/ 호르무즈, 두 번째 음절에 강세

sentences.original: Although the world's dependence on oil has diminished since the Arab embargo of 1973 and the Iranian revolution of 1979, when crude was still commonly burned to generate electricity, this apparent resilience is deceptive, for today's oil demand -- concentrated in transport and petrochemicals -- is far more inelastic, meaning that prices must climb steeply to bring consumption into line with a supply disruption of this unprecedented magnitude, one that some analysts suggest could push crude beyond $150 a barrel if the strait remains closed.
sentences.translation: 세계의 석유 의존도는 원유가 발전용으로 흔히 소비되던 1973년 아랍 석유 금수 조치와 1979년 이란 혁명 이래 줄어들었지만, 이러한 외견상의 회복력은 기만적이다. 오늘날 석유 수요는 운송과 석유화학에 집중되어 있어 훨씬 더 비탄력적이기 때문이며, 이는 이처럼 전례 없는 규모의 공급 차질에 소비를 맞추려면 가격이 급격히 올라야 한다는 것을 뜻한다 -- 해협이 계속 봉쇄되면 원유가 배럴당 150달러를 넘을 수 있다고 일부 분석가는 전망한다.

grammarElements.text: Although the world's dependence on oil has diminished
grammarElements.label: 양보 부사절

grammarElements.text: for today's oil demand ... is far more inelastic
grammarElements.label: 이유절 (for)

vocabulary.meaning: 비탄력적인 (가격이 변해도 수요가 크게 변하지 않는)
vocabulary.example: Demand for essential medicines is highly inelastic.

expressions.usage: 공급과 수요의 불균형을 해소하는 과정을 설명할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: Although [apparent improvement], [reality] is deceptive, for [true reason]
patterns.explanation: 표면적 긍정을 인정한 뒤 실제로는 그렇지 않음을 밝히는 양보-반전 패턴
examples: Although unemployment has fallen, the recovery is deceptive, for most new jobs are part-time.
Although inflation has eased, the calm is deceptive, for core prices remain sticky.

drillQuestions.question: Although the world's dependence on oil has diminished, this apparent resilience is ______, for today's oil demand is far more inelastic.

sentences.notes: Although-but-for 구조로 '석유 의존도 감소'라는 낙관론을 논파하는 경제학적 논증. inelastic라는 경제 용어가 핵심.

koreanAlignment.en: the world's dependence on oil has diminished
this apparent resilience is deceptive
today's oil demand is far more inelastic
koreanAlignment.ko: 오늘날 석유 수요는 훨씬 더 비탄력적이다

koreanAlignment.en: prices must climb steeply

sentences.original: The damage extends well beyond oil, reaching into commodities that few outside specialist trading desks had associated with the Persian Gulf: Qatar's liquefied natural gas hub at Ras Laffan, which supplied nearly a fifth of global LNG before a drone strike forced its closure, has dragged European gas prices up by more than half, while the loss of the facility's helium output -- roughly a third of worldwide supply and an irreplaceable coolant in semiconductor manufacturing -- threatens to bottleneck the very technology sector on which future economic growth depends.
sentences.translation: 피해는 석유를 훨씬 넘어서, 전문 트레이딩 데스크 밖에서는 페르시아만과 연관 짓는 사람이 거의 없었던 원자재들에까지 미친다. 드론 공습으로 폐쇄되기 전까지 세계 LNG의 거의 5분의 1을 공급하던 카타르 라스라판의 액화천연가스 허브는 유럽 가스 가격을 절반 이상 끌어올렸고, 이 시설의 헬륨 생산 손실 -- 세계 공급량의 약 3분의 1이자 반도체 제조에 대체 불가능한 냉각재 -- 은 미래 경제 성장이 의존하는 바로 그 기술 부문의 병목을 초래할 위험이 있다.

grammarElements.text: reaching into commodities that few ... had associated with the Persian Gulf
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (확장)

grammarElements.text: threatens to bottleneck the very technology sector
grammarElements.label: 두 번째 주절 동사

vocabulary.example: A shortage of chips could bottleneck the entire auto industry.

expressions.usage: 문제의 범위가 예상보다 훨씬 넓음을 강조할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Problem] extends well beyond [obvious area], reaching into [unexpected area]
patterns.explanation: 문제의 범위가 명백한 영역을 넘어 예상 밖의 영역까지 미침을 보여주는 패턴
examples: The scandal extends well beyond politics, reaching into business and media.
The drought extends well beyond agriculture, reaching into energy production and manufacturing.

drillQuestions.question: The damage extends well beyond oil, reaching into commodities that few outside specialist trading desks had ______ with the Persian Gulf.

sentences.notes: 콜론(:)을 사용해 추상적 주장을 구체적 사례(카타르 LNG, 헬륨)로 전환하는 고급 저널리즘 기법. bottleneck을 동사로 쓴 점도 주목.

koreanAlignment.en: The damage extends well beyond oil
commodities that few outside specialist trading desks had associated with the Persian Gulf
koreanAlignment.ko: 전문 트레이딩 데스크 밖에서는 거의 연관 짓지 않던 원자재들

koreanAlignment.en: an irreplaceable coolant in semiconductor manufacturing
the very technology sector on which future economic growth depends
koreanAlignment.ko: 미래 경제 성장이 의존하는 바로 그 기술 부문

sentences.original: Refined fuel markets, squeezed from both ends by the near-disappearance of Gulf crude and the inability of the region's mega-refineries to export, have deteriorated so sharply that Asian processors have cut throughput by up to 15%, China has suspended all refined-product exports, and modelling by the data firm Kpler suggests that, should Hormuz stay blocked, Oceania will exhaust 80% of its jet-fuel stocks within 36 days and poorer Asian nations will face critical petrol shortages in under a fortnight.
sentences.translation: 정제유 시장은 페르시아만 원유의 거의 소멸과 역내 대형 정유소의 수출 불능이라는 양쪽에서 압박을 받아 급격히 악화되었다. 아시아 정유사들은 처리량을 최대 15%까지 줄였고, 중국은 모든 정제유 수출을 중단했으며, 데이터 기업 Kpler의 모델링에 따르면 호르무즈가 계속 봉쇄될 경우 오세아니아는 36일 이내에 항공유 재고의 80%를 소진하고 빈곤한 아시아 국가들은 2주 안에 심각한 휘발유 부족에 직면할 것이라 한다.

grammarElements.label: 도치된 조건절 (= if Hormuz should stay blocked)

vocabulary.example: The refinery reduced throughput after supplies were disrupted.

expressions.usage: 공급과 수출 양쪽이 동시에 막혀 이중 압박을 받는 상황을 설명할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Market], squeezed from both ends by [cause A] and [cause B], has deteriorated so sharply that [consequences]
patterns.explanation: 양면 압박과 그 결과의 심각성을 한 문장에 담는 패턴
examples: The housing market, squeezed from both ends by rising rates and falling supply, has deteriorated so sharply that first-time buyers have all but vanished.
The labour market, squeezed from both ends by automation and immigration curbs, has deteriorated so sharply that wages remain stagnant.

drillQuestions.question: Refined fuel markets, squeezed from both ends, have deteriorated so sharply that Asian processors have cut ______ by up to 15%.

sentences.notes: so ... that 결과절 안에 세 가지 구체적 결과를 병렬로 나열. should 도치 조건절은 격식체에서 if를 대체하는 고급 문법.

koreanAlignment.en: Refined fuel markets, squeezed from both ends
the near-disappearance of Gulf crude
have deteriorated so sharply that
should Hormuz stay blocked
in under a fortnight

sentences.original: Manufacturing, the second pillar of global industry now buckling under the strain, depends on Gulf petrochemical plants for nearly 45% of seaborne naphtha flows and up to 30% of key plastic feedstocks -- a dependence so acute that several Asian plastic-makers have already declared force majeure, while pharmaceutical supply chains stretching from China's bulk chemical importers to India's generic-drug factories confront shortages of the active compounds that underpin everything from aspirin to antibiotics.
sentences.translation: 지금 압박에 휘어지고 있는 글로벌 산업의 두 번째 기둥인 제조업은 해상 나프타 물동량의 거의 45%와 주요 플라스틱 원료의 최대 30%를 페르시아만 석유화학 공장에 의존하고 있다 -- 그 의존도가 너무 심각하여 여러 아시아 플라스틱 제조사가 이미 불가항력을 선언했고, 중국의 벌크 화학 수입업체에서 인도의 제네릭 의약품 공장까지 이어지는 제약 공급망은 아스피린에서 항생제까지 모든 것을 떠받치는 활성 화합물의 부족에 직면하고 있다.

grammarElements.text: the second pillar of global industry now buckling under the strain
grammarElements.label: 동격 삽입구

vocabulary.meaning: 불가항력 (계약 이행이 불가능한 천재지변이나 전쟁 등)
vocabulary.example: The supplier declared force majeure after the earthquake destroyed its warehouse.

expressions.usage: 시스템이나 구조가 과부하로 붕괴 직전임을 묘사할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Sector], [appositive description], depends on [source] for [percentage] -- a dependence so acute that [consequence]
patterns.explanation: 특정 부문의 의존도를 수치로 제시한 뒤 그 심각성을 결과절로 보여주는 패턴
examples: Europe, the continent most exposed to Russian energy, depended on Moscow for 40% of its gas -- a dependence so acute that sanctions required years of preparation.
The tech sector depends on Taiwan for over 90% of advanced chips -- a dependence so acute that any disruption would halt global electronics production.

drillQuestions.question: Several Asian plastic-makers have already declared force ______, due to the acute dependence on Gulf petrochemical plants.

sentences.notes: 동격 삽입구(the second pillar...)가 문장에 맥락을 더하고, force majeure라는 법률/무역 용어가 사태의 심각성을 전문적으로 표현한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Manufacturing, the second pillar of global industry
koreanAlignment.ko: 글로벌 산업의 두 번째 기둥인 제조업

koreanAlignment.en: now buckling under the strain
a dependence so acute that
pharmaceutical supply chains
the active compounds that underpin everything from aspirin to antibiotics
koreanAlignment.ko: 아스피린에서 항생제까지 모든 것을 떠받치는 활성 화합물

pronunciationNotes: force majeure: /fɔːrs mæˈʒɜːr/ 프랑스어 기원, '마쥐어'로 발음

sentences.original: The aluminium market illustrates how a single chokepoint can ripple through the industrial world: with Qatar's mega-smelter starved of gas, Bahraini and Emirati plants unable to export, and shipping costs from Oman's port soaring under the threat of attack, three-month delivery prices on the London Metal Exchange have surged to near four-year highs, hammering the packaging, transport, and renewable-energy sectors that consume the metal in vast quantities.
sentences.translation: 알루미늄 시장은 하나의 병목이 어떻게 산업 세계 전체에 파급될 수 있는지를 잘 보여준다. 카타르의 대형 제련소가 가스 부족에 시달리고, 바레인과 에미리트의 공장들이 수출 불능 상태이며, 오만 항구의 운송 비용이 공격 위협 아래 치솟으면서, 런던금속거래소의 3개월 인도 가격은 거의 4년 만의 최고치까지 급등해 이 금속을 대량으로 소비하는 포장, 운송, 재생에너지 부문을 강타하고 있다.

grammarElements.text: how a single chokepoint can ripple through the industrial world
grammarElements.label: 명사절 목적어

grammarElements.text: with Qatar's ... Bahraini ... and shipping costs ...
grammarElements.label: with + 독립분사구문 (세 가지 병렬)

vocabulary.example: The canal is a chokepoint for global shipping.

expressions.usage: 하나의 사건이 연쇄적으로 여러 분야에 영향을 미칠 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Case] illustrates how [cause] can ripple through [system]: with [condition A], [condition B], and [condition C], [result]
patterns.explanation: 구체적 사례로 연쇄 파급 효과를 설명하는 패턴. 콜론 뒤에 with 분사구문으로 조건을 나열한 뒤 결과를 제시
examples: The chip shortage illustrates how a single factory fire can ripple through global manufacturing: with auto plants idled, electronics delayed, and prices soaring, recovery took over a year.
The port strike illustrates how one chokepoint can ripple through the economy: with containers stranded, shelves emptying, and costs climbing, retailers scrambled for alternatives.

drillQuestions.question: The aluminium market illustrates how a single ______ can ripple through the industrial world.

sentences.notes: 콜론 뒤 with 독립분사구문 세 개를 병렬로 배치해 인과관계의 복잡한 사슬을 시각적으로 보여준다.

koreanAlignment.en: The aluminium market illustrates
how a single chokepoint can ripple through the industrial world
koreanAlignment.ko: 하나의 병목이 산업 세계에 어떻게 파급될 수 있는지를

koreanAlignment.en: three-month delivery prices on the London Metal Exchange
hammering the packaging, transport, and renewable-energy sectors

sentences.original: Perhaps most ominously for the world's poorest nations, the war threatens global food production, given that a third of seaborne fertiliser trade -- chiefly urea derived from natural gas and phosphate -- transits the Strait of Hormuz, and countries such as Sudan, which sources over half its fertiliser from the Gulf, face the prospect of catastrophic shortages just as spring planting begins across the northern hemisphere.
sentences.translation: 아마도 세계 최빈국들에 가장 불길한 것은, 이 전쟁이 세계 식량 생산을 위협한다는 점이다. 해상 비료 무역의 3분의 1 -- 주로 천연가스에서 추출한 요소와 인산염 -- 이 호르무즈 해협을 통과하며, 비료의 절반 이상을 페르시아만에서 조달하는 수단 같은 나라들은 북반구 전역에서 봄 파종이 시작되는 바로 이 시점에 재앙적 부족 사태에 직면하고 있다.

grammarElements.text: Perhaps most ominously for the world's poorest nations
grammarElements.label: 문두 부사구

grammarElements.text: given that a third of seaborne fertiliser trade transits the Strait
grammarElements.label: 이유를 나타내는 분사절

vocabulary.example: The sky darkened ominously before the storm.

expressions.usage: 앞으로 닥칠 부정적 상황을 경고할 때 사용하는 격식 표현

patterns.pattern: Perhaps most ominously, [subject] threatens [consequence], given that [evidence]
patterns.explanation: 일련의 문제 중 가장 심각한 것을 마지막에 강조하며 제시하는 패턴
examples: Perhaps most ominously, the policy threatens democratic norms, given that emergency powers have rarely been revoked once granted.
Perhaps most ominously, the spill threatens the drinking water supply, given that three reservoirs sit downstream of the leak.

drillQuestions.question: Perhaps most ______ for the world's poorest nations, the war threatens global food production.

sentences.notes: Perhaps most ominously로 시작해 기사의 감정적 정점을 찍는다. 식량 위기라는 인도주의적 차원으로 논의를 확장.

koreanAlignment.en: Perhaps most ominously for the world's poorest nations
koreanAlignment.ko: 아마도 세계 최빈국들에 가장 불길한 것은

koreanAlignment.en: a third of seaborne fertiliser trade
chiefly urea derived from natural gas and phosphate
just as spring planting begins across the northern hemisphere
koreanAlignment.ko: 북반구 전역에서 봄 파종이 시작되는 바로 이 시점에

sentences.original: Even the world's skies have contracted, as the conflict has rendered the airspace above the Persian Gulf all but impassable -- reducing Dubai's departures to a third of normal levels and Doha's to a mere 5% -- and forced airlines onto costly detours that add hours to east-west routes already lengthened by the closure of Russian airspace after the invasion of Ukraine, shrinking the corridor of passable sky to a sliver above Azerbaijan.
sentences.translation: 세계의 하늘마저 수축했다. 이 분쟁이 페르시아만 상공을 거의 통과 불가능하게 만들어 두바이의 출발편을 정상의 3분의 1로, 도하를 겨우 5%로 줄였고, 우크라이나 침공 이후 러시아 영공 폐쇄로 이미 길어진 동서 노선에 수 시간을 더하는 값비싼 우회 경로로 항공사들을 내몰면서, 통과 가능한 하늘의 회랑을 아제르바이잔 위의 좁은 조각으로 줄여 버렸다.

grammarElements.text: as the conflict has rendered the airspace all but impassable
grammarElements.label: 이유 부사절

grammarElements.text: shrinking the corridor of passable sky to a sliver above Azerbaijan
grammarElements.label: 결과 분사구문

vocabulary.example: Heavy snow made the mountain road impassable.

expressions.usage: 완전히는 아니지만 사실상 그러한 상태임을 표현하는 고급 수식어

patterns.pattern: Even [unexpected domain] has [negative change], as [conflict] has rendered [space] all but [adjective]
patterns.explanation: 전쟁의 영향이 예상 밖의 영역까지 미쳤음을 Even으로 강조하는 패턴
examples: Even the internet has slowed, as the conflict has rendered undersea cables all but unusable.
Even neutral ports have emptied, as the blockade has rendered shipping lanes all but closed.

drillQuestions.question: The conflict has rendered the airspace above the Persian Gulf all but ______, reducing Dubai's departures to a third of normal levels.

sentences.notes: Even으로 시작해 '하늘마저 수축'이라는 강력한 이미지를 제시. sliver(조각)라는 단어가 항공 회랑의 극단적 축소를 시각적으로 전달한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Even the world's skies have contracted
rendered the airspace all but impassable
forced airlines onto costly detours
shrinking the corridor of passable sky to a sliver above Azerbaijan
koreanAlignment.ko: 통과 가능한 하늘의 회랑을 아제르바이잔 위의 좁은 조각으로 줄여 버렸다

sentences.original: Central banks now confront a policy nightmare in which the inflationary pressures of soaring energy costs collide with the recessionary drag of supply-chain paralysis, leaving politicians to weigh the politically irresistible lure of energy subsidies -- which exceeded 2.5% of GDP in many European countries during the 2022 crisis and would, if repeated, deepen sovereign debts while shifting the worst pain to developing nations in Asia and Africa.
sentences.translation: 중앙은행들은 이제 치솟는 에너지 비용의 인플레이션 압력이 공급망 마비의 경기 침체 압력과 충돌하는 정책적 악몽에 직면해 있으며, 정치인들은 에너지 보조금이라는 정치적으로 거부할 수 없는 유혹을 저울질해야 하는 처지에 놓였다 -- 2022년 위기 때 많은 유럽 국가에서 GDP의 2.5%를 넘었던 이 보조금이 되풀이된다면, 국가 부채를 심화시키는 동시에 최악의 고통을 아시아와 아프리카의 개발도상국에 전가하게 될 것이다.

grammarElements.text: a policy nightmare in which ... collide with ...
grammarElements.label: 목적어 + 관계절

vocabulary.example: High interest rates risk triggering recessionary forces across the eurozone.

expressions.usage: 서로 양립 불가능한 두 경제적 힘이 동시에 작용할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Institution] confronts a [crisis] in which [force A] collides with [force B], leaving [actors] to [difficult choice]
patterns.explanation: 두 가지 상충하는 압력 사이에 끼인 정책 딜레마를 묘사하는 패턴
examples: The Fed confronts a dilemma in which taming inflation collides with protecting employment, leaving policymakers to choose between two painful options.
Regulators confront a paradox in which promoting innovation collides with ensuring safety, leaving consumers to bear the risk.

drillQuestions.question: Central banks now confront a policy nightmare in which the inflationary pressures of soaring energy costs collide with the ______ drag of supply-chain paralysis.

sentences.notes: 인플레이션과 경기 침체라는 두 상충 압력(스태그플레이션)을 collide라는 충돌 은유로 표현. 정책 딜레마의 본질을 한 문장에 압축.

koreanAlignment.en: Central banks now confront a policy nightmare
inflationary pressures collide with recessionary drag
koreanAlignment.ko: 인플레이션 압력이 경기 침체 압력과 충돌한다

koreanAlignment.en: the politically irresistible lure of energy subsidies
koreanAlignment.ko: 에너지 보조금이라는 정치적으로 거부할 수 없는 유혹

koreanAlignment.en: shifting the worst pain to developing nations

sentences.original: What the war has made inescapably clear is that an extraordinary share of the world's supply chains -- in oil, gas, petrochemicals, metals, fertiliser, and even the helium that cools the magnets inside chip-fabrication equipment -- funnels through a 54-kilometre-wide channel alongside a belligerent state, and that the era of assuming this vulnerability could be safely ignored has, with devastating abruptness, come to an end.
sentences.translation: 이 전쟁이 피할 수 없이 분명하게 만든 것은, 석유, 가스, 석유화학, 금속, 비료, 심지어 칩 제조 장비 내부의 자석을 냉각하는 헬륨에 이르기까지 세계 공급망의 엄청난 비중이 교전국 옆의 54킬로미터 폭 수로를 통과한다는 사실이며, 이 취약성을 안전하게 무시할 수 있다는 가정의 시대가 파괴적인 급작스러움과 함께 끝났다는 것이다.

grammarElements.text: What the war has made inescapably clear
grammarElements.label: 명사절 주어 (what절)

grammarElements.text: that an extraordinary share ... funnels through
grammarElements.label: 보어 (첫 번째 that절)

grammarElements.text: and that the era of assuming ... has come to an end
grammarElements.label: 보어 (두 번째 that절)

vocabulary.example: The belligerent state refused all offers of mediation.

expressions.usage: 하나의 시대나 상태가 완전히 종료되었음을 선언할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: What [event] has made inescapably clear is that [revelation A], and that [revelation B]
patterns.explanation: 사건이 드러낸 두 가지 불편한 진실을 병렬 that절로 제시하는 결론 패턴
examples: What the pandemic has made inescapably clear is that healthcare systems were underfunded, and that globalised supply chains carry hidden fragilities.
What the crisis has made inescapably clear is that regulation lagged behind innovation, and that the costs of inaction far exceed the costs of reform.

drillQuestions.question: What the war has made inescapably clear is that an extraordinary share of the world's supply chains funnels through a 54-kilometre-wide channel alongside a ______ state.

sentences.notes: 기사의 결론부. What절 주어와 이중 that절 보어로 전체 논지를 요약. 'with devastating abruptness'라는 삽입구가 마지막 감정적 울림을 더한다.

koreanAlignment.en: What the war has made inescapably clear
koreanAlignment.ko: 이 전쟁이 피할 수 없이 분명하게 만든 것은

koreanAlignment.en: an extraordinary share of the world's supply chains
funnels through a 54-kilometre-wide channel alongside a belligerent state
the era of assuming this vulnerability could be safely ignored
koreanAlignment.ko: 이 취약성을 안전하게 무시할 수 있다는 가정의 시대

koreanAlignment.en: with devastating abruptness, come to an end
